1898	  inverse pushforward-composition factor collapses to the identity
1899	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}) and the congruence factor collapses to the
1900	  identity (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom}), leaving the unit factor

1902	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',\operatorname{Spec}\varphi})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) coherence for step~(iii).

1904	  \emph{Step (iii): the mate-unwinding crux, as a chain of clean-term links.} After expanding the
1905	  bare \((g')\)-unit (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}), the comparison
1906	  composite is reduced to \(\rho\) by chaining the four clean-term links below. Each is an equality
1907	  between two freshly elaborated terms, so the \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module instance diamond is absent in
1908	  its own statement and it is provable by ordinary functoriality / isomorphism cancellation; the
1909	  links chain by transitivity of equality:

1918	      the codomain read

1920	    \item \(L_4\) evaluates the lone surviving affine unit as \(\rho\)

1967	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor,

1971	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs} at the literal pullback projection legs
1972	  \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\): with the codomain read
1973	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} being definitionally the variable-legs read at
1974	  those legs, the concrete statement follows directly. The remainder of this proof records the
1975	  mathematical content of that abstract identity.
1976	
1977	  Throughout, let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the
1978	  canonical isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}, and
1979	  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
1980	  inclusions, so that by Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} the two projection legs
1981	  factor as
1982	  \[
1983	    g' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A,
1984	    \qquad
1985	    f' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'} .
1986	  \]
1987	  The inner composite \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) is the four-factor map
1988	  \[
1989	    f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr) \;\circ\;
1990	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g', f})^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
1991	    (\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
1992	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f', g})^{\mathrm{inv}},
1993	  \]
1994	  with \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
1995	  \(\eta^{g'}\) the unit of the \((g')\)-adjunction, and
1996	  \(w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) the commutativity of the square.
1997	
1998	  \emph{Why a direct substitution fails.} The identity cannot be obtained by substituting the
1999	  leg factorizations directly, because the legs \(g'\) and \(f'\) appear in multiple positions
2000	  simultaneously: as the index of the adjunction \(\eta^{g'}\), inside the equality proof
2001	  \(w\) parametrizing \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w\), inside the argument
2002	  \(f'_{*}\bigl((g')^{*}\widetilde M\bigr)\) of the codomain comparison
2003	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain
2004	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
2005	  The reindex is therefore restructured into three sub-steps so that the legs become explicit
2006	  free variables; it is \emph{not} a single rewrite.
2007	
2008	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
2009	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
2010	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
2011	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
2012	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
2013	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
2014	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
2015	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
2016	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
2017	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
2018	
2019	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
2020	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
2021	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
2022	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
2023	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
2024	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
2025	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
2026	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
2027	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
2028	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
2029	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
2030	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
2031	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
2032	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
2033	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
2034	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
2035	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
2036	  sections.
2037	
2038	  \emph{(iii) Reduction to Seam~1 via the leg-reindex engine.} With the legs substituted
2039	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
2040	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
2041	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), rewrites the unit factor
2042	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the
2043	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})\) coherence, into the affine
2044	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the
2045	  \(e\)-unit. Since \(e\) is an isomorphism of schemes, its pullback functor is an equivalence
2046	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an
2047	  isomorphism absorbed into the codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). The surviving
2048	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit has section value over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) equal to
2049	  the algebraic unit \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Seam~1
2050	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Composing the surviving factor with the
2051	  codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
2052	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) and reading over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
2053	  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) identifies all four factors with the restriction of
2054	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely
2055	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\)
2056	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}) — the
2057	  ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is
2058	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
2059	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.
2060	\end{proof}

2479	\begin{lemma}
2480	
2481	\leanok
2482	[(B) Generator close: the base change of $\rho$ is the inverse regrouping]
2483	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
2484	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_gstar_generator_close}
2485	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2486	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'
2487	  % (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the "boils down to the equality" step
2488	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L933--938).
2489	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality
2490	  % $$ (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M $$ as $R'$-modules."
2491	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
2492	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2493	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2494	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2495	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2496	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2497	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2498	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2499	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2500	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2501	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2502	  \[
2503	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2504	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2505	  \]
2506	\end{lemma}
2507	\begin{proof}
2508	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2509	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2510	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2511	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2512	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2513	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2514	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2515	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2516	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2517	\end{proof}

2529	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} (reading \(g^*\) of a tilde as extension of scalars along
2530	  \(\psi\), built from \(\gamma_{\psi}\), Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}), and

2533	  counit on either side, equals the algebraic counit: as a single transport identity,
2534	  \[

2538	  \]

2541	  denotes the tilde--\(\Gamma\) counit. This is the counit dual of the unit-across-conjugate

2549	  \(\mathrm{adj}_L = (\widetilde{(-)}_R \dashv \Gamma_R) . (g^* \dashv g_*)\) and
2550	  \(\mathrm{adj}_R = (\,{}^{\psi}\!(-) \dashv {}_{\psi}(-)) . (\widetilde{(-)}_{R'} \dashv
2551	  \Gamma_{R'})\), with the right-adjoint comparison \(\gamma_{\psi}\)
2552	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}). The conjugate of \(\gamma_{\psi}\) is, at every base
2553	  module, the inverse of \(\Pi_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}), as in Seam~1.
2554	  Splitting each composite counit into its tilde--\(\Gamma\) factor and its geometric / algebraic
